# Documention And The Importance Of It

Without documentation there would be a lot more problems to deal with. When you write a documentation for your project your future self and others may end up reading it and understanding it with the given explanations from your document even when time passes.
If that documentation did not exist then most people would end up leaving and the ones that stay will end up having to piece your work together.

One thing to make sure of is to not make a bad documentation. A bad documentation is worse than not having one at all.

# The Three Level of Documentation:
When most people hear the word documentation, they imagine a single big blob of text that sits next to a project. But in real software development, documentation actually exists on three different levels, each serving a different purpose and audience. Having an understanding of these levels helps you write clearer code, collaborate better, and make your projects easier for others to use. 

These levels being:
- Project-level docs -- help people get started
- Code-level docs -- help people understand the implementation
- In-context docs -- help people understand the evolution and reasoning

This is just a simple explanation of the three levels if you want to know more you will have to look up some more indepth sources.
